Log Settings

Stillwater writes a structured log of everything it does -- scans, rule fixes, API calls, errors. These controls govern where logs are written, what severity is captured, and how aggressively they're rotated to keep disk usage bounded. Read more →

Stillwater emits structured log lines for every meaningful event (scans, rule fixes, API calls, errors). These settings decide how chatty those logs are, what format they use, and whether they are kept on disk in addition to stdout. Changes take effect immediately.

Minimum severity to emit. Info is the recommended default. Debug and Trace are verbose and intended for diagnosing specific issues; avoid leaving them on in production. Read more →
JSON output is structured and easy to ingest into log shippers or search tools. Text is human-readable plain lines, better for direct inspection. Read more →

Log to file

When on, Stillwater writes a rotating log file in addition to stdout. Configure the path, size limit, and rotation policy in the fields below. Read more →

Write logs to a rotating file in addition to stdout.

Where the rotating log file is written. Use a path inside your persistent config directory so logs survive container restarts. Read more →

Logs are always written to stdout. This enables an additional rotating file.

The active log file is rotated once it reaches this size. The old file becomes a numbered archive and a new file is started. Read more →
How many rotated log files to keep on disk alongside the active log. Older archives are removed during rotation once this count is exceeded. Read more →
Rotated log files older than this many days are removed, regardless of the files-to-keep count. Both limits apply independently. Read more →
